• *[2 other completed tasks – not listed]* | – | – | | **Canceled** (1) | • iDEAL payment method (updated 2026‑03‑20 17:00) | – | – | --- ## 2. Active‑Task Health Check | Task | Time since last update* | **Stalled?** ( > 4 h no progress) | Acceptance‑criteria present? | Flag | |------|--------------------------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------|------| | **Stripe webhook handler** | ~ 4 h (08:30 → current) | **No** | Not explicit in description | **⚠️ Clarify AC** | | **PayPal SDK integration** | 2 days + 23 h | **Yes** | Not explicit | **⚠️ Potentially stalled – blocked** | | **Currency conversion service** | 3 days + ≈ 11 h | **Yes** | Not explicit | **⚠️ Potentially stalled** | \*All timestamps are UTC. **Observations** * The two older active tasks have had no activity for > 4 h (in fact days) and lack clear acceptance criteria – both are flagged as *potentially stalled*. * The Stripe webhook task is progressing (signature verification passed) but still needs explicit acceptance criteria. --- ## 3. Planned‑Task Readiness | Planned Task | Current priority (relative to completed work) | Ready to become **Active**? | Relevance | Recommendation | |--------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|-----------|----------------| | **Refund flow implementation** (updated 2026‑03‑22) | High – refunds are required for EU launch and depend on completed Stripe & PayPal modules | Yes – prerequisites largely in place | Core to payment lifecycle | **Activate** | | *Planned Task #2* (unspecified) | Unknown – need review | Unknown | Unknown | **Review** | | *Planned Task #3* (unspecified) | Unknown – need review | Unknown | Unknown | **Review** | | *Planned Task #4* (unspecified) | Unknown – need review | Unknown | Unknown | **Review** | **Irrelevant / Cancellation Check** * No planned task appears obsolete from the information given. The only cancelled item is **iDEAL payment method**, already marked as canceled. --- ## 4. Demand Alignment & Activation Priorities | Demand (status) | Related tasks | Dependency / priority impact | |-----------------|---------------|------------------------------| | **Multi‑currency support** (in_progress) – updated 2026‑03‑23 08:30 | Currency conversion service (active) | Critical for EU launch; conversion service must be un‑blocked. | | **Payment provider integration** (in_progress) – updated 2026‑03‑22 14:00 | Stripe webhook, PayPal SDK (both active) | Both providers must be fully integrated before refunds can be processed. | | **Fraud detection module** (open) – updated 2026‑03‑18 09:00 | *No task yet* | High‑impact future work; should be scoped soon. | **Top‑3 Planned Tasks to Activate Next** (based on demand priority, dependencies, and overall project timeline): 1. **Refund flow implementation** – directly needed once Stripe & PayPal are live. 2. **Fraud detection module** – currently open demand; start early to avoid later bottlenecks. 3. *[Third planned task]* – whichever of the remaining planned items has the strongest link to the “Payment provider integration” demand (e.g., “Support for Apple Pay” or “PCI‑DSS compliance checklist”). *If the third planned task is not yet defined, schedule a grooming session to identify the highest‑value item.* --- ## 5. Blockers & Responsible Resources | Blocked Task | Impediment | Owner / Responsible | |--------------|------------|----------------------| | **PayPal SDK integration** | Missing PayPal sandbox credentials – cannot test integration. | **Mike** (assigned to request access). | | **Currency conversion service** | Caching strategy still TBD; no decision on how to store ECB feed. | *Team lead* (assign a dev to finalize caching design). | | **Refund flow implementation** (planned) | Not yet active – awaiting clearance of the two provider integrations. | *Product Owner* to prioritize activation after provider blockers are cleared. | --- ## 6. Summary Metrics | Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | **Active tasks** | 3 | | **Potentially stalled active tasks** | 2 (PayPal SDK integration, Currency conversion service) | | **Completed tasks since last review (≥ 2026‑03‑20 15:00)** | 2 (Unit tests for Stripe module, Receipt email template) | | **Planned tasks ready for activation** | 1 confirmed (Refund flow) + up to 2 pending clarification | | **Blocked tasks** | 2 (PayPal SDK, Currency conversion) | --- ## 7. Recommended Next Actions (Actionable Checklist) 1. **Unblock PayPal SDK integration** - Mike to request sandbox credentials **by 2026‑03‑24 12:00**. - Once received, assign a dev to resume integration and add acceptance criteria. 2. **Define Acceptance Criteria** for all active tasks (Stripe webhook, PayPal SDK, Currency conversion). - Product Owner to draft clear “Done” definitions **by 2026‑03‑25** and attach to each task. 3. **Finalize Caching Strategy** for the Currency conversion service. - Architecture lead to propose a design (in‑memory vs Redis) **by 2026‑03‑26**

Adjust any that are now lower‑impact. | | **Readiness to become active** | Verify that each planned task has:
• A well‑defined description
• Acceptance criteria
• No outstanding dependencies (e.g., design specs, API contracts). | | **Irrelevant tasks** | Identify any tasks whose deliverable is already covered by completed work or that no longer align with the current product vision. Recommend cancellation with a brief justification (e.g., “Feature X superseded by Y”). | | **Top‑3 activation candidates** | Based on the two open demands (Self‑service portal features, Navigation & UX overhaul) and known dependencies, the following types of planned work are likely highest‑value:
1. **Self‑service feature back‑end services** (e.g., “Self‑service API endpoints”)
2. **UX mock‑ups & design hand‑off for navigation overhaul**
3. **Dashboard analytics widgets** (to complement the component library). Confirm exact task IDs and move them to **Active** after acceptance criteria are added. | --- ## 4. Recommended Next Actions (Actionable Checklist) 1. **Clarify Acceptance Criteria** - For each of the four active tasks, add explicit acceptance criteria to the task description by **2026‑03‑25**. - Assign the task owner (e.g., component lead, navigation lead, API lead, SSO lead) to draft and get stakeholder sign‑off. 2. **Resolve Blockers** - **Design Team:** Deliver final component API specs for the Dashboard library. - **Vendor / Integration Team:** Escalate IdP metadata 503 issue; aim for a stable endpoint. 3. **Re‑evaluate Priorities of Planned Tasks** - Conduct a quick priority‑review meeting (≤ 30 min) with Product Owner and Scrum Master to: a. Confirm which planned tasks are still needed. b. Adjust priorities based on completed work and current demand. 4. **Activate Top‑3 Planned Tasks** - Identify the concrete task IDs for: a. Self‑service API development b. Navigation UX design hand‑off c. Dashboard analytics widgets - Move them to **Active** status **once** acceptance criteria are in place (target: **2026‑03‑28**). 5. **Consider Cancellation of Irrelevant Work** - Review the full list of 9 planned tasks; flag any that duplicate functionality already delivered (e.g., legacy navigation components). - Propose cancellation with a brief rationale (e.g., “Superseded by new navigation refactor”). 6. **Update Stakeholders** - Share this report with the project team (Product Owner, Scrum Master, Development Leads) by **2026‑03‑24**. - Schedule a short stand‑up to confirm ownership of the above actions. --- ### Quick Summary for the Team - **Active tasks:** 4 (all stalled, all missing acceptance criteria). - **Stalled count:** 4 → immediate

• **User‑preference API** – last update 2026‑03‑16 12:00, no comment. | Medium – tasks have been idle for > 7 days, risking schedule slip. | Create a **“Investigate Stuck Tasks”** task (owner: team lead) and request a status update within 24 h. | | **Orphaned Demands** (in‑progress demand with zero active tasks) | Mapping between tasks and demands is not provided, so we cannot confirm whether any in‑progress demand lacks active work. | Unknown – could hide bottlenecks. | Add demand‑to‑task linkage in the tracking system; run the orphan‑check in the next health‑check. | | **Resource Contention** (multiple agents on same demand) | No agent roster, so contention cannot be assessed. | Unknown. | Ensure agents are recorded per task; run contention analysis next cycle. | | **Agent Health** (inactive/unhealthy agents) | No agent data supplied. | Unknown. | Populate the agent register (people, bots, services) and include health flags in future reports. | | **Demand Activity** | At least one demand is in‑progress; no flag needed. | – | – | --- ## 5. Overall Status - **Degraded** – The endeavour is functional, but there are **medium‑severity issues** (stuck tasks) and **information gaps** (agent list, demand‑task mapping) that prevent a full health assessment. --- ## 6. Recommended Immediate Actions 1. **Create a “Investigate Stuck Tasks” ticket** (priority = high). - Assign to the owners of *Navigation refactor* and *User‑preference API*. - Require a status comment and next‑step plan within 24 h. 2. **Populate the Agent Register**: - List every person, bot, or service assigned to the endeavour, with current availability/status. - Attach the register to the next health‑check. 3. **Link Tasks to Demands**: - Add a field in the task tracker that references the parent demand. - This will enable detection of orphaned demands and resource contention. 4. **Schedule a short sync (≤ 30 min)** with the demand owners to confirm that work is progressing on the two in‑progress demands and to surface any hidden blockers. 5. **Update the health‑check documentation** to capture the above data points for the next run (agent health, demand‑task linkage, timestamps of last comments). --- ### Summary for the Project Team - **Current health:** *Degraded* – work is moving forward but two active tasks have stalled > 2 h without any comment. - **Critical next step:** Open a “Investigate Stuck Tasks” ticket and obtain updates within the next day. - **Data gaps:** Agent list and task‑to‑demand mapping are missing; fill these to enable full automated health monitoring. Addressing the stuck tasks and completing the missing metadata will likely restore the endeavour to a **healthy** state.

## 10. Narrative Zusammenfassung (für den Projekt‑Owner)

Im Berichtszeitraum wurden drei zentrale Dokumentationsaufgaben erfolgreich abgeschlossen, wodurch die REST‑API‑Dokumentation nun vollständig ist und bereits gemerged wurde. Die einzige noch aktive Aufgabe – die Erstellung der MCP‑Tool‑Referenzseiten – liegt bereits seit über einem Tag in Arbeit und sollte priorisiert werden, um den Sprint‑Zielwert von 100 % Abdeckung zu erreichen. Die Durchsatzrate bleibt im Vergleich zur Vorperiode stabil, und alle beteiligten Ressourcen zeigen kontinuierliche Aktivität. Es wird empfohlen, zusätzliche Ressourcen oder automatisierte Unterstützung für die verbleibenden MCP‑Seiten bereitzustellen, um den Abschluss des Sprints termingerecht zu sichern.
I’m sorry, but I can’t comply with that.
